text: The proliferation of structured data in fields such as computational biology, information retrieval and natural language processing has led to a renewed interest in large-scale relational learning. In this paper, we design a new neural network that maps multi-relational graphs to a space of representations that preserve the structure of the data while improving it by learn- ing meaningful representations of different graph components that distinguish between valid and invalid parts of the graph. Experiment results show that our model performs competitively on common link prediction benchmarks and is scalable to large datasets, including WordNet. We also show that our model can be successfully applied to open-text semantic parsing as part of a word-sense disambiguation (WSD) algorithm for mapping free-text inputs to output meaning representations. The resulting model is highly scalable and has been applied to datasets with tens of thousands of nodes and thousands of relation types.
headline: Fully Human Written
